ranges.label: Very Low Wellbeing
ranges.description: Your subjective well-being is very low across multiple life domains including standard of living, health, achievements, and relationships. This level of dissatisfaction is associated with significant distress and may indicate depression or other mental health concerns requiring professional attention.
suggestions: Seek support from a mental health professional to explore underlying causes of low well-being
Identify one or two life domains where small, achievable improvements are possible
Connect with trusted friends, family, or community resources for social support
Establish basic self-care routines including regular sleep, nutrition, and physical activity

ranges.label: Low Wellbeing
ranges.description: Your well-being is below average, suggesting dissatisfaction in several life domains. While not critically low, this pattern may affect your motivation, relationships, and overall quality of life. Targeted improvements in specific areas can meaningfully boost your well-being.
suggestions: Review which specific life domains scored lowest and focus improvement efforts there
Set realistic goals for areas you have most control over, such as health behaviors or social connections
Consider counseling to address persistent sources of dissatisfaction
Practice gratitude exercises to build awareness of positive aspects in your life
Engage in activities that provide a sense of purpose and accomplishment

ranges.label: Moderate Wellbeing
ranges.description: Your well-being is in the moderate range, reflecting reasonable satisfaction across most life domains with room for growth. Most people fall in this range. Identifying specific areas for enhancement can help you move toward a more fulfilling life experience.
suggestions: Identify your strongest life domains and leverage them to improve weaker areas
Invest in relationships and community involvement, which strongly predict well-being
Pursue meaningful goals aligned with your personal values
Maintain healthy lifestyle habits that support both physical and psychological well-being

ranges.label: High Wellbeing
ranges.description: Your subjective well-being is high, indicating satisfaction across multiple life domains. You perceive your standard of living, health, achievements, relationships, safety, and community connectedness positively. This level of well-being is a strong protective factor for mental and physical health.
suggestions: Continue the habits and relationships that contribute to your high well-being
Share your positive outlook by supporting others in your community
Stay open to new experiences and challenges that sustain personal growth
